title: luzula erecta var. calabra
intro: Luzula erecta var. calabra is a variant of the Luzula erecta plant, commonly known as the woodrush, a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Juncaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, with its roots and rhizomes possessing medicinal properties that have been valued for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The health benefits associated with Luzula erecta var. calabra include its ability to aid in the treatment of urinary tract issues, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to limited scientific research on its efficacy and potential side effects.
